nodes.title: Mad/Sad/Glad: Team members share what made them mad, sad, or glad during the project. It uncovers emotional responses and areas for improvement.
Star Wars: Use the Force to reflect on the project. Discuss the 'Dark Side' (challenges) and 'Light Side' (successes). Encourages a fun, engaging discussion.
Harry Potter: Sort issues into Hogwarts houses. Gryffindor for courage, Slytherin for cunning solutions, etc. Sparks creativity and teamwork.
Halloween Spooktacular: Identify 'tricks' (problems) and 'treats' (successes). Adds a playful twist to problem-solving.
Monopoly: Discuss the project as if it were a game. What were the 'Chance' cards (unexpected events) and 'Community Chest' (team support)? Encourages strategic thinking.
Escape Room: Identify 'puzzles' (challenges) that were solved and those that remain. Promotes problem-solving and collaboration.
Space Odyssey: Explore the project as a space mission. What were the 'launch successes' and 'asteroid obstacles'? Inspires futuristic thinking.
Under the Sea: Dive into the depths of the project. Discuss 'treasures' (successes) and 'sharks' (risks). Encourages deep analysis.
Winter Wonderland: Reflect on 'snowflakes' (unique ideas) and 'snowstorms' (challenges). Adds a seasonal touch to reflections.
Superheroes: Identify team 'superpowers' and 'kryptonite'. Encourages recognizing strengths and addressing weaknesses.
Fantasy Quest: Discuss the project as a quest. Identify 'dragons' (challenges) and 'treasures' (successes). Promotes imaginative problem-solving.
TV Show Marathon: Relate project phases to episodes in a series. Discuss 'cliffhangers' (unresolved issues) and 'plot twists' (unexpected changes).
So you want to build a retro board?
Not sure what theme to use? Vote below!
Start/Stop/Continue: A classic theme where team members discuss what to start doing, stop doing, and continue doing. It helps in identifying actionable items and maintaining good practices.
New Year Resolutions: Reflect on past achievements and set new goals for the team. Encourages forward-thinking and planning.
Road Trip: Map out the project journey. Discuss 'pit stops' (achievements) and 'detours' (challenges). Encourages a holistic view of the project.
drag these onto your board where you want input

Superhero Retro Board Action Items

Superpowers (What Went Well)

Owner: Scrum Master
Priority: Medium
Steps:

  1. Document successful team collaboration practices
  2. Recognize team members who demonstrated exceptional skills
  3. Implement identified best practices in future sprints

Kryptonite (What Didn't Go Well)

Owner: Project Manager
Priority: High
Steps:

  1. Identify root causes of blockers
  2. Create mitigation plan for recurring issues
  3. Schedule follow-up meeting to review solutions

Temp Check

Owner: Team Lead
Priority: Low
Steps:

  1. Collect anonymous feedback on team morale
  2. Summarize key emotional indicators
  3. Address concerning patterns in next retrospective

Future Missions (What To Try Next)

Owner: Product Owner
Priority: Very High
Steps:

  1. Prioritize improvement ideas from team
  2. Select top 2-3 actionable items
  3. Assign owners and deadlines for implementation
  4. Track progress in next sprint planning

How did this sprint make you feel?
Under the Sea Sprint Retrospective Board
Deep Dive Discoveries: What went well during our sprint?
Great collaboration between design and development teams on the dashboard UI.
Daily stand-ups were focused and productive this sprint.
Rough Waters: What challenges did we face?
Integration with the third-party API took longer than expected.
Unexpected server downtime affected our testing schedule.
Requirements for the reporting feature changed mid-sprint.
Sailing Forward: What can we improve for next time?
Break down larger tasks into smaller, more manageable pieces.
Schedule more time for code reviews to catch issues earlier.
Improve documentation for onboarding new team members.
Set up automated testing for critical components.
How do you feel about this sprint? Drag your fish to indicate your sentiment (1-5)
